appraisal_events.evidence: « Elles étaient devenues obéissantes à mes caprices… de laquelle je n'étais pas médiocrement fier d'avoir cueilli, dérobé à tous, la plus belle rose. » / « En dormant, elle était devenue une plante… En le tenant sous mon regard, dans mes mains… sa vie m'était soumise. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator frames Albertine (and her circle) as de-mystified, compliant, and, when asleep, reduced to a plant-like, fully possessed body. This locally lowers her agency and stature relative to his control.

status_effects.explanation: Albertine is locally diminished as the narrator describes her as obedient, dehumanized in sleep, and wholly subjected to his possession.

ambiguities: The narrator reports his own sense of possession and uses metaphorical dehumanization; the text may carry an ironic distance, making full endorsement of this diminishment uncertain.
